creator: rodrigo@bebik.net
text:
Hi Olivier,

I analyze the Pr you submit with different configurations and using
the most common releases and I finally come to  the conclusion the
problem was caused by the kernel version you are using (use clang
or gcc  doesn't affect the result).

As said before I was able to reproduce the problem in a virtualbox with
a current kernel and you know 'current' is not really known for his 
stability,
specially so far from the next release cycle.

So, I believe spending time investigating this issues is not really 
relevant,
so if you don't have very good reasons to run applications out of a 
"production
ready" environment, I suggest edwin can close this issue as is.

Best regards,

- rodrigo
